sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Girona FC have struggled recently, with only one win in their last five matches, managing just 1.2 goals per game and conceding an average of 1.4. Real Madrid, on the other hand, are in strong form, remaining unbeaten in their last three matches, scoring an impressive 2.4 goals per game while conceding 1.2 on average. This stark contrast in form heavily favors Real Madrid ahead of the clash at Estadi Montilivi.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Real Madrid have dominated Girona FC, securing victories in both matches with clean sheets (2-0 and 3-0). Historically, Real Madrid have won 4 of the last 5 head-to-heads, often scoring first and controlling the game, which aligns with their current momentum.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Girona FC, sitting 18th in the league with 11 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 25 goals this season (19th in La Liga), while scoring just 12. Real Madrid, 3rd with 32 points, boast 28 goals scored and only 12 conceded, showcasing their attacking prowess and defensive solidity, particularly through key players like Kylian Mbappé (13 goals, league leader).

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Girona will rely on forwards like Vladyslav Vanat (3 goals) to challenge Real Madrid’s defense, but missing Daley Blind could hurt their backline. Real Madrid miss Éder Militão and Dean Huijsen, yet their attack remains lethal with Mbappé and Vinícius Júnior (5 goals, 4 assists) in form, supported by high-rated midfielders like Arda Güler.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Real Madrid’s superior form, historical dominance, and attacking firepower, coupled with Girona’s defensive vulnerabilities and low league position, an away win seems highly likely. Sofascore’s prediction (84% away win) aligns with the data, suggesting Real Madrid will control possession and create more scoring opportunities. Expect a comfortable victory for the visitors.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Girona FC leads Real Madrid 1-0 in a match that has seen both teams create opportunities at Estadi Montilivi. Girona capitalized on a late first-half strike by Azzedine Ounahi to take the advantage just before the whistle. Real Madrid, despite dominating possession and creating a big chance, failed to convert their opportunities into goals. The home side showed resilience in defense while Real Madrid appeared frustrated after missing key moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Real Madrid held the edge in possession and made significantly more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to Girona. However, their finishing let them down with a notable big chance missed. Girona, on the other hand, was more clinical with their limited opportunities, securing the lead through a well-taken goal. Defensively, Girona outperformed Real Madrid in duels, winning a much higher percentage, which helped them disrupt the away side's rhythm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Girona, Azzedine Ounahi stood out with his goal and overall impact in midfield, earning a high rating at the break. Viktor Tsygankov also played a crucial role by providing the assist and contributing to dangerous attacks. On Real Madrid's side, Kylian Mbappé was instrumental in creating chances, including a big opportunity, but lacked the final touch. Jude Bellingham showed tenacity in midfield with tackles and interceptions, while Éder Militão came close to scoring but missed a significant chance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Girona started with intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes with consistent attacking spells. Real Madrid responded with a prolonged period of dominance, maintaining pressure for a significant stretch in the middle of the half. However, Girona regained control towards the end of the half with renewed attacking energy, culminating in their goal. Real Madrid had moments of intense pressure late on, but couldn't break through before the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Girona's strategy focused on absorbing pressure and countering effectively, which paid off with their goal. Their high success in duels allowed them to regain possession in key areas and launch attacks. Real Madrid, despite their possession-based approach, struggled to penetrate Girona's compact defense consistently. The away side may need to adjust by increasing their tempo and focusing on quicker transitions to exploit gaps in the second half.
